content_markdown: Carbon has a relative atomic mass of 12 and oxygen has a relative atomic mass of 16. A sample of $6\text{ g}$ of carbon has twice as many atoms as

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

vibe_explanation: First, calculate the number of moles of carbon: $n = \frac{m}{M} = \frac{6}{12} = 0.5\text{ mol}$. Since the question states this sample has twice as many atoms as the oxygen sample, the oxygen sample must contain $0.25\text{ mol}$ of atoms. To find the mass of oxygen atoms, use $m = n \times M = 0.25 \times 16 = 4\text{ g}$.
